member.name: T:TextGame.GameBuilder
member.summary: Utility class for building and loading text adventure games
member.remarks: Provides static methods to: - Load games from JSON cartridge files - Build a sample game for testing and demonstration - Discover available cartridges in a directory This class serves as the main entry point for game creation and loading.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameBuilder.LoadFromCartridge(System.String)
member.summary: Loads a game from a JSON cartridge file.
member.param: Path to the JSON cartridge file
member.returns: Starting room or null if load fails
member.remarks: This method delegates to CartridgeLoader.LoadFromJson() to handle JSON deserialization and game graph construction.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameBuilder.BuildSampleGame
member.summary: Builds the default sample game (Haunted Mansion). This method is kept for backward compatibility.
member.returns: The starting room of the sample game
member.remarks: Creates a hardcoded game with: - 4 rooms: Entrance Hall, Library, Garden, Cellar - 4 items: key, lamp, statue, book This method demonstrates programmatic game creation and provides a quick way to test the game engine without loading a cartridge.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameBuilder.FindCartridges(System.String)
member.summary: Finds all cartridge files in the specified directory.
member.param: Directory to search for cartridges
member.remarks: Searches recursively for all .json files in the specified directory. Useful for implementing cartridge browsing and selection UI. If the directory doesn't exist, it will be created.

member.name: T:TextGame.GameEngine
member.summary: Core game engine for text-based adventure games
member.remarks: The GameEngine class manages all game state including the current room, player inventory, and game running status. It processes player commands, handles room navigation, item interactions, and generates text output through an event-driven architecture.

member.name: E:TextGame.GameEngine.OutputGenerated
member.summary: Event raised when the game generates output text
member.remarks: Subscribe to this event to receive all game output including room descriptions, command responses, error messages, and help text. This allows for flexible output handling (console, GUI, logging, etc.).

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the GameEngine class
member.remarks: Creates empty room and inventory dictionaries and sets the game state to not running. Call Start() with a starting room to begin gameplay.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.Start(TextGame.Room)
member.summary: Starts the game at the specified starting room
member.param: The room where the game begins
member.remarks: Sets the current room, marks the game as running, displays a welcome message, and describes the starting room. This should be called before processing any commands.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.ProcessCommand(System.String)
member.summary: Processes a player command
member.param: The command string entered by the player
member.remarks: Parses the input string and executes the appropriate action. Supported commands include: go/move, look, take/get, inventory/inv, use, examine, help, quit/exit. Commands are case-insensitive and multi-word item names are supported.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.Move(System.String)
member.summary: Moves the player in the specified direction
member.param: The direction to move (north, south, east, west, up, down)
member.remarks: Checks if the current room has an exit in the specified direction. If valid, moves to the new room and describes it. Otherwise, displays an error message.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.DescribeCurrentRoom
member.summary: Displays the description of the current room
member.remarks: Outputs the room name, description, list of visible items, and available exits. This is called when entering a room or when the player uses the 'look' command.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.TakeItem(System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to take an item from the current room
member.param: The name of the item to take
member.remarks: Searches for the item in the current room (case-insensitive). If found and takeable, moves it to inventory. Otherwise, displays appropriate error message.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.ShowInventory
member.summary: Displays the player's current inventory
member.remarks: Lists all items currently carried by the player. If inventory is empty, displays an appropriate message.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.UseItem(System.String)
member.summary: Uses an item from the player's inventory
member.param: The name of the item to use
member.remarks: Checks if the item exists in inventory and displays its use description. The item remains in inventory after use (unless removed by game logic).

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.ExamineItem(System.String)
member.summary: Examines an item in detail
member.param: The name of the item to examine
member.remarks: Searches for the item in both inventory and current room. Displays the item's detailed description if found.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.ShowHelp
member.summary: Displays the help message with available commands
member.remarks: Lists all supported commands with brief descriptions of their usage. This is shown when the player types 'help'.

member.name: M:TextGame.GameEngine.Output(System.String)
member.summary: Sends output to subscribers of the OutputGenerated event
member.param: The message to output
member.remarks: Internal method used to generate all game output through the event system.

member.name: P:TextGame.GameEngine.IsRunning
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the game is currently running
member.value: true if the game is running; otherwise, false
member.remarks: The game is running after Start() is called and until the player quits.

member.name: T:TextGame.Item
member.summary: Represents an object in the game world
member.remarks: Items can be examined, taken (if CanTake is true), used, and carried in inventory. Items can be either portable objects or fixed scenery depending on the CanTake property.

member.name: P:TextGame.Item.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the item
member.value: The item's name as used in commands and displayed to the player
member.remarks: This is the identifier used when players reference the item in commands. Item name matching is case-insensitive.

member.name: P:TextGame.Item.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the detailed description of the item
member.value: A description shown when the player examines the item
member.remarks: This provides additional narrative detail about the item's appearance, history, or potential uses.

member.name: P:TextGame.Item.UseDescription
member.summary: Gets or sets the description displayed when the item is used
member.value: Text shown when the player uses this item from inventory
member.remarks: Defaults to "Nothing happens." if not specified. This can describe the result of using the item or provide hints about its purpose.

member.name: P:TextGame.Item.CanTake
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the item can be taken
member.value: true if the item can be added to inventory; otherwise, false
member.remarks: Set to false for scenery, fixed objects, or items that are too heavy to carry. Set to true for portable items that can be collected and used.

member.name: M:TextGame.Item.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Item class
member.param: The name of the item
The detailed description of the item
Whether the item can be taken (default: true)
Description when item is used (default: "Nothing happens.")
member.remarks: Creates a new item with the specified properties. By default, items are takeable and have a generic use description.

member.name: T:TextGame.Loaders.CartridgeLoader
member.summary: Handles loading and parsing of JSON cartridge files
member.remarks: The CartridgeLoader provides functionality to: - Load complete games from JSON files - Load only cartridge metadata for browsing - Discover available cartridges in a directory - Build runtime game objects from cartridge data

member.name: M:TextGame.Loaders.CartridgeLoader.LoadFromJson(System.String)
member.summary: Loads a game from a JSON cartridge file
member.param: Path to the cartridge JSON file
member.returns: The starting room of the game, or null if loading fails
member.remarks: This method reads the JSON file, deserializes it to a GameCartridge object, builds the complete game graph (rooms, items, connections), and returns the starting room. Any errors during loading are logged to the console.

member.name: M:TextGame.Loaders.CartridgeLoader.BuildGameFromCartridge(TextGame.Models.GameCartridge)
member.summary: Builds a game graph from a GameCartridge data model
member.param: The cartridge data to build from
member.returns: The starting room, or null if the starting room ID is invalid
member.remarks: This method constructs the runtime game objects in the following order: 1. Create all Item objects from ItemData 2. Create all Room objects from RoomData 3. Populate rooms with their items 4. Establish room connections (exits) 5. Return the starting room This order prevents circular reference issues.

member.name: M:TextGame.Loaders.CartridgeLoader.LoadCartridgeMetadata(System.String)
member.summary: Loads only the metadata from a cartridge file
member.param: Path to the cartridge JSON file
member.returns: The GameCartridge metadata object, or null if loading fails
member.remarks: This is useful for browsing available cartridges without building the full game. It allows quick access to name, description, author, and version information.

member.name: M:TextGame.Loaders.CartridgeLoader.FindCartridges(System.String)
member.summary: Finds all JSON cartridge files in a directory
member.param: Directory path to search
member.returns: List of paths to cartridge JSON files
member.remarks: Searches recursively for all .json files in the specified directory. If the directory doesn't exist, it will be created and an empty list returned.

member.name: T:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge
member.summary: Data model representing a complete game cartridge
member.remarks: This class is used for JSON serialization/deserialization of game cartridges. It contains all metadata, rooms, and items required to build a complete game.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the game
member.value: The display name of the game

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the description of the game
member.value: A brief summary of the game's story or theme

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Author
member.summary: Gets or sets the author's name
member.value: The name of the person or team who created this game

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Version
member.summary: Gets or sets the version number
member.value: Version number following semantic versioning (e.g., "1.0.0")

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.StartingRoomId
member.summary: Gets or sets the ID of the starting room
member.value: The room ID where the game begins
member.remarks: Must match the Id of one of the rooms in the Rooms list.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Rooms
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of rooms in the game
member.value: Collection of room definitions

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.GameCartridge.Items
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of items in the game
member.value: Collection of item definitions

member.name: T:TextGame.Models.RoomData
member.summary: Data model representing a room definition
member.remarks: This class is used for JSON serialization of room data. It contains references to connected rooms and items rather than object instances.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.RoomData.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this room
member.value: A unique string identifying this room
member.remarks: Used as a reference in exit definitions and the starting room ID.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.RoomData.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the display name of the room
member.value: The room's name as shown to the player

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.RoomData.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the description of the room
member.value: A narrative description of the room's appearance

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.RoomData.Exits
member.summary: Gets or sets the exits from this room
member.value: Dictionary mapping direction names to room IDs
member.remarks: Keys are direction strings (e.g., "north", "down"). Values are room IDs that must match other rooms in the cartridge.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.RoomData.ItemIds
member.summary: Gets or sets the items present in this room
member.value: List of item IDs found in this room
member.remarks: Item IDs must match items defined in the cartridge's Items list.

member.name: T:TextGame.Models.ItemData
member.summary: Data model representing an item definition
member.remarks: This class is used for JSON serialization of item data.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.ItemData.Id
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier for this item
member.value: A unique string identifying this item
member.remarks: Used as a reference in room item lists.

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.ItemData.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the display name of the item
member.value: The item's name as used in commands

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.ItemData.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the detailed description of the item
member.value: Description shown when examining the item

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.ItemData.CanTake
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the item can be taken
member.value: true if the item can be added to inventory; otherwise, false

member.name: P:TextGame.Models.ItemData.UseDescription
member.summary: Gets or sets the description shown when using the item
member.value: Text displayed when the player uses this item

member.name: T:TextGame.Room
member.summary: Represents a location in the game world
member.remarks: A Room contains a name, description, connections to other rooms (exits), and items present in the room. Rooms form the graph structure of the game world.

member.name: P:TextGame.Room.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the display name of the room
member.value: The room's name as displayed to the player

member.name: P:TextGame.Room.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets the narrative description of the room
member.value: A description of the room's appearance and atmosphere

member.name: P:TextGame.Room.Exits
member.summary: Gets or sets the available exits from this room
member.value: Dictionary mapping direction names (e.g., "north") to connected rooms
member.remarks: Keys are direction strings (case-insensitive). Values are the Room objects that can be reached by traveling in that direction.

member.name: P:TextGame.Room.Items
member.summary: Gets or sets the items present in this room
member.value: Dictionary mapping item names to Item objects
member.remarks: Items can be examined, taken (if CanTake is true), or used by the player. When an item is taken, it is removed from this dictionary.

member.name: M:TextGame.Room.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Room class
member.param: The display name of the room
The narrative description of the room
member.remarks: Creates a room with empty exits and items dictionaries. Use AddExit() and AddItem() to populate the room.

member.name: M:TextGame.Room.AddExit(System.String,TextGame.Room)
member.summary: Adds an exit from this room to another room
member.param: The direction name (e.g., "north", "down")
The destination room
member.remarks: Direction names are automatically converted to lowercase for consistency. If an exit already exists in that direction, it will be replaced.

member.name: M:TextGame.Room.AddItem(TextGame.Item)
member.summary: Adds an item to this room
member.param: The item to add
member.remarks: The item is added using its Name property as the dictionary key. If an item with the same name already exists, it will be replaced.

member.name: M:TextGame.Room.RemoveItem(System.String)
member.summary: Removes an item from this room
member.param: The name of the item to remove
member.remarks: Typically called when a player takes an item. If the item doesn't exist, the method has no effect (no error is raised).
